long_stay: High-level Italian language courses lasting more than 90 days
Nationally recognized vocational/professional training courses (corsi di formazione professionale), including socio-health training
short_stay: Short-term programs under 90 days (Type C visa)
qualifying_courses.notes: The student must prove they will follow a course in an educational institution or professional training on a full-time basis, in line with their previous studies in their home country.

booking_methods.description: Primary and official platform, centrally managed by the Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation; used for visas, passports, citizenship, and other consular services; adopted by most consulates worldwide since June 2021. Requires individual account registration, OTP verification, and appointment confirmation 3-10 days before the scheduled date. Waiting list available.
Used by certain consulates as an alternative or supplementary channel, particularly for visa applications in specific countries.

steps.title: Get accepted into an accredited Italian institution
Book an appointment at your local Italian consulate/embassy
Prepare and submit all required documents
Pay the visa fee and submit fingerprints
Wait for processing
steps.description: Processing can take up to 90 days.
steps.action_required: Applicants are strongly advised to apply at least 3 months before their planned departure.

steps.title: Collect your passport with the visa affixed

required_documents.name: Valid passport
required_documents.details: Must be valid for at least three months beyond your intended stay and contain at least two blank pages.

required_documents.name: Official letter of acceptance from an accredited Italian educational institution
Proof of accommodation in Italy
required_documents.details: E.g., university housing confirmation, rental agreement, or host declaration.

required_documents.name: Round-trip flight reservation or proof of travel itinerary
A cover letter or statement of purpose
required_documents.details: Explaining your study plans in Italy.

required_documents.name: Pre-enrollment summary from the Universitaly portal

financial_requirements.reference_basis: Italian Ministry of University and Research (MUR) for academic years 2025/2026 and 2026/2027
acceptable_proof: Personal bank statements (covering the last 3–6 months, signed and stamped by the bank, not older than 30 days at time of submission)
Parental or guardian bank statements, accompanied by a notarized sponsorship/affidavit letter confirming financial commitment, proof of relationship, and the sponsor's proof of employment or income
A scholarship award letter from the Italian government, a recognized Italian university, or another official body, clearly stating the amount covered and duration
A financial guarantee from an Italian institution, university, or a citizen legally residing in Italy
An education loan sanction letter from an accredited bank, provided the funds are deposited and visible on submitted bank statements
Income Tax Returns (ITR) of the sponsor, demonstrating consistent and legitimate income history
Fixed deposit certificates, accompanied by bank confirmation of accessibility
Proof of tuition fee payment in full (may reduce the remaining funds requirement)

residence_permit.booking_method: Poste Italiane post office with a Sportello Amico counter
required_documents: Mod. 209 Modulo 1 filled out (for study purposes, only Modulo 1 is required)
Photocopy of valid passport (data pages and study visa page)
Copy of the study programme, admission letter, or enrollment certificate, stamped by the Italian diplomatic/consular representation at the time the visa was issued
Proof of sufficient financial means (bank/post statement or certificate from institution confirming scholarship amount); minimum threshold is the annual Italian social allowance (€7,101.12 for 2026)
Copy of health/accident insurance policy valid in Italy for the entire duration of the permit (travel and tourist insurance policies are not accepted)
Copy of codice fiscale (Italian tax identification number) - not mandatory for first issue
residence_permit.notes: Fees payable at the post office: €16.00 (marca da bollo), €30.46 (electronic permit issuance fee), €30.00 (postal service fee), €40.00 (administrative contribution for permits of up to 1 year) or €50.00 (administrative contribution for permits of 1–2 years). Legal basis: Art. 39, Legislative Decree No. 286/1998 and Arts. 44-bis, 45, and 46, DPR No. 394/1999.

restrictions: Hours exceeding 20 hours per week or 1,040 hours per year are not permitted under the student residence status.
work_while_studying.notes: Applies to both employed and self-employed work.

required_documents: Mod. 209 Modulo 1 (Modulo 2 only if the student also has income from work)
Certificate proving the passing of at least 1 exam for the 1st renewal, and at least 2 exams for each subsequent renewal (for university students)
Documentation of continued enrollment from the accredited training institution (for professional training students)
Updated proof of financial means for the duration of the renewed permit
Updated health/accident insurance policy valid in Italy for the renewed period
Updated university enrollment certificate (certificato di iscrizione)
permit_renewal.notes: Renewal application must be submitted to the Questore of the province of residence. Same fees apply as for the first issue. Processing typically takes 90–180 days or more. The postal receipt (ricevuta) serves as proof of legal status. Travel to other Schengen countries during processing is not permitted with the receipt alone; direct travel to the country of citizenship is allowed.

post_study_work.notes: A study residence permit can be converted into either a work permit or a self-employment permit. Applications for conversion can be submitted at any time of the year, with no limit on the number, following the Cutro Decree.

common_mistakes.mistake: Booking the embassy appointment too late
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Book immediately after receiving your acceptance letter — slots fill fast
Prepare everything in Italian or English; apostille where required
Ensure at least €6,947.33 is clearly visible in bank statements (€534.41 × 13 months)

common_mistakes.mistake: Missing the 8-day deadline for residence permit
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Apply for the Permesso di Soggiorno at a post office within 8 working days of arriving in Italy
Complete the Universitaly pre-enrollment before visiting the consulate

common_mistakes.mistake: Letting the permit expire without renewal
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Start the renewal process at least 60 days before expiration
